text:
While originally designed for natural language processing, the self-attention mechanism has recently swept through the field of computer vision. However, applying self-attention to images presents three significant challenges: (1) treating 2D images as 1D sequences ignores essential spatial structures; (2) the quadratic computational complexity is prohibitive for high-resolution inputs; and (3) standard self-attention captures spatial dependencies but neglects channel-wise adaptability. In this paper, we introduce Large Kernel Attention (LKA), a novel linear attention mechanism designed to provide the self-adaptive, long-range correlations of self-attention while circumventing its inherent flaws. By decomposing large-kernel convolutions, LKA achieves a massive receptive field and multi-dimensional adaptability with high efficiency.

Building on this module, we present the Visual Attention Network (VAN). Despite its architectural simplicity, VAN delivers exceptional performance across a broad spectrum of visual tasks, including image classification, object detection, semantic segmentation, and pose estimation. Experimental results demonstrate that VAN consistently outperforms well-known CNNs and Vision Transformers (ViTs) of similar scale. For instance, VAN-B6 achieves 87.8% accuracy on ImageNet-1K and sets a new state-of-the-art in panoptic segmentation with 58.2 PQ. Furthermore, VAN-B2 significantly surpasses Swin-T on the ADE20K (50.1 vs. 46.1 mIoU) and COCO (48.8 vs. 46.2 AP) benchmarks. This work offers a powerful, simple, and versatile baseline for the computer vision community. Code and models are available at https://github.com/Visual-Attention-Network.
